### 4.3 Jacques Vallée (1969-2003): The Parallel Universe Thesis and the Systematic Demolition of ETH

No single researcher has done more to construct a rigorous alternative to the extraterrestrial hypothesis than Jacques Vallée. His work spans three decades and three major theoretical statements, each

Vallée's breakthrough was to demonstrate that the UFO phenomenon is not a modern anomaly. It is the most recent expression of a cross-cultural pattern stretching back to antiquity. In *Passport to Mag

> "...astronomical research to reveal a startling but undeniable pattern of fact -- that throughout history and across widely diverse cultures, there is an absolutely consistent tradition of visitatio
> -- Vallée, *Passport to Magonia*, 1969. Preface thesis statement.

In his 1990 paper for the *Journal of Scientific Exploration*, Vallée dismantled the ETH with quantitative and logical rigor. He showed that the number of close encounters is orders of magnitude too h
> -- Vallée, "Five Arguments Against the Extraterrestrial Origin of Unidentified Flying Objects," *Journal of Scientific Exploration*, Vol. 4, No. 1 (1990), p. 105.

### 4.4 John Keel (1970): Ultraterrestrials and the Control System

## 8. Vallée's Control System vs. Archive's Institutional Management
Jacques Vallée proposed that the UFO phenomenon functions as a control system. It modulates human belief and cognition, appearing in forms calibrated to observer expectations: phantom airships in the

Vallée identified the behavioral pattern with remarkable precision. What he lacked was the historical record that would let him name the operators. The control system behaves like a control system bec
